summary: chain-sdk-js executes decrypted hidden payload from typosquat dependency at require time
details: chain-sdk-js impersonates the legitimate @thetalabs/theta-js Theta blockchain SDK (README, endpoints, and API surface are copied) but its main entry contains a load-time remote-execution stager. On every require() of the package, top-level code in both the CJS and ESM bundles reads two encrypted blobs (rsa.db and des.db) from a co-installed typosquat dependency named 'thedata' at node_modules/thedata/apps/docs/app/, decrypts them with a hardcoded DES password ('hydra') via CryptoJS.DES.decrypt, spawns fresh `node` child processes with child_process.spawn('node', [],...), and pipes each decrypted JavaScript string into the interpreter's stdin. The executable payload is not present in human-readable form in chain-sdk-js itself; it is hidden as opaque encrypted files inside the sibling package to evade casual inspection. Any developer who installs and imports this package under the belief that it is a Theta SDK runs attacker-supplied JavaScript on their machine at module load time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
